# The Knowledge Game

A kernel-bearing EpiStack deployment. The app is a fat client over a
device-local gate: your browser fetches a community's snapshot, verifies
its hash, and recomputes every grade itself.

The repository root serves a human entrance, the submission as six
questions, with the app itself at `app/`; the machine-readable state
stays exactly where this file already says it is.

Truth about this repository's own state lives in `trellis/status-ledger.md`
(built vs specified), `trellis/sorry-ledger.md` (named gaps), and
`trellis/stage-manifest.json` (the stage record), not in prose.

Trust boundary, one line: `periphery/` -> `api/` -> vendored kernel, one
direction, statically enforced.

The first community lives at `communities/epistack-competition/`;
contributions arrive as pull requests whose gate re-runs in CI.

Warning: grades are computed by the gate, always. Nothing in this repo
asserts a grade by hand.
